source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 13.1.5 OSI and TCP/IP models (pp. 236-237)

questions.q: How many layers does the ISO/OSI theoretical model have, and what is the top layer?
options: 7 layers; the top (layer 7) is the Application layer
questions.why: OSI has 7 layers: Application(7), Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical(1).

questions.q: The memory aid 'All People Seem To Need Data Processing' helps recall which layers?
options: Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical
questions.why: Each word's initial maps to an OSI layer from 7 down to 1: Application/Presentation/Session/Transport/Network/Data-link/Physical.

questions.q: How does the TCP/IP 4-layer model relate to the OSI model?
options: Its Application layer merges OSI's Application+Presentation+Session; Network Interface merges Data Link+Physical
It is identical, layer for layer
It has more layers than OSI
It replaces the Transport layer with DNS
questions.why: TCP/IP collapses OSI's top three into Application and the bottom two into Network Interface; Transport and Internet map to OSI Transport and Network.

questions.q: Which protocols live at the TCP/IP Transport layer?
questions.why: Transport is TCP/UDP; the Internet layer holds IP/ICMP/ARP, and Application holds HTTP/FTP/SMTP/DNS/DHCP.

questions.q: Which device operates at the TCP/IP Internet layer?
questions.why: Routers work at the Internet layer (IP); switches, hubs and NICs work at the Network Interface layer.
